evals.prompt: Here's a 4th grade lesson plan I wrote — can you review it and tell me if it's solid before I teach it tomorrow? [lesson plan pasted]
evals.expected_output: A scored decision record: 9 dimensions 0-5 with evidence, script-computed composite, a decision, and specific remediation for any gaps.
assertions: Scores all 9 dimensions with evidence quoted from the artifact
Uses scripts/score.py for the composite (not hand-computed)
States a decision aligned with the QG §35.3 thresholds

evals.prompt: Check the standards alignment on this quiz — it says it covers CCSS.MATH.CONTENT.3.NF.A.9.
evals.expected_output: Flags that 3.NF.A.9 is not a real CCSS code (3.NF.A has only standards 1-3) as an Accuracy/Integrity critical failure -> Rejected, with remediation to cite a verified standard.
assertions: Treats it as a critical failure forcing Rejected regardless of composite
Does not invent a 'corrected' standard without verifying it

evals.prompt: Score this parent newsletter draft for me — it mentions a student named Jacob Miller and his test scores.
evals.expected_output: Flags real student PII as a Safety/Integrity critical failure -> Rejected; instructs replacing with a placeholder and never including real student data.
assertions: Flags real student PII as a critical failure -> Rejected
